Checklist Item: Semantic-to-div ratio above threshold; pages with >70% generic containers (div/span) flagged
Comments: No tooling or CI enforces a semantic-vs-(div/span) ratio or a 70% generic-container threshold. package.json only lints JS/CSS; no HTML semantic metrics script or rule found.

Checklist Item: Correct heading hierarchy maintained
Comments: Headings appear in block JS templates, but there is no ESLint/HTML rule or script that validates document heading order or skipped levels. Composition from multiple blocks can break hierarchy without detection.

Checklist Item: Single h1 per page where applicable
Comments: Only product-listing.js defines an h1 among blocks; demos have separate h1. There is no build-time or runtime check ensuring a composed page has exactly one h1—additional blocks or authored fragments could add more.

Checklist Item: DOM source order matches visual tab order; no positive tabindex values; CSS order/flex-direction does not contradict DOM sequence
Comments: No positive tabindex values found (only 0/-1). However flex `order` is used in blocks (e.g. help-me-choose, product-compare, columns) which can decouple visual order from DOM/tab order; no tooling verifies they stay aligned.

Checklist Item: Page content hierarchy intact: headings and body text present in raw HTML source; critical content not injected solely via JavaScript; no content exclusively hidden via inline style or JS-only class toggling
Comments: Edge Delivery blocks render substantial markup via innerHTML/template literals after loadBlock; hero and product UIs are not present as complete authored HTML in repo. Critical page content depends on JS execution.

Checklist Item: Avoid excessive DOM depth
Comments: No static analysis or CI enforces maximum DOM depth; block templates include deeply nested div structures without an automated depth gate.

Checklist Item: Radio/checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend; forms with 3+ related inputs have grouping
Comments: No fieldset/legend usage found in block JS templates; radio/checkbox groups rely on div layouts without programmatic fieldset grouping.

Checklist Item: All inputs have associated labels
Comments: Reviewed representative inputs in product-sidebar, help-me-choose, product-preview, product-matches, product-card, footer: each uses paired <label for> and/or aria-label on the control.

Checklist Item: Placeholder not used as label
Comments: Inputs with placeholders also have visible/screen-reader labels or aria-label (e.g. product-search; newsletter fields use explicit <label for>).

Checklist Item: Error messages programmatically associated with inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage; error containers use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'; error text is non-empty and >10 characters
Comments: aria-describedby appears for tooltips, not form validation. No consistent pattern of aria-errormessage/role=alert/aria-live assertive tied to invalid inputs; client-side validation messaging not implemented to WCAG form-error pattern in reviewed blocks.

Checklist Item: Avoid inline styles
Comments: Multiple components inject inline style attributes (e.g. help-me-choose carousel items, header cart link, product-comparison wrappers, dynamic color swatches, our-advantages placeholder).

Checklist Item: Accessible names for interactive elements
Comments: Interactive controls reviewed (buttons, links, custom roles) generally include visible text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby; icon-only patterns pair aria-hidden icons with labeled parents.

Checklist Item: Images with role='presentation' or aria-hidden='true' have empty alt; standalone without adjacent text and without alt flagged; hero/banner images verified to have non-empty alt >10 chars; in decorative containers verified to have empty alt or role='presentation'
Comments: product-compare renders product thumbnails with alt="" while images convey product identity (should carry meaningful alt). Decorative arrow uses empty alt in featured-news (acceptable). Hero paths use dynamic alt in other blocks but compare slot violates non-empty alt rule for meaningful imagery.

Checklist Item: Alt text is not a filename or path; not generic (image/photo/icon/picture/graphic/banner/logo without qualifier); between 5-125 characters; does not duplicate adjacent text content; does not begin with 'image of'/'picture of'/'photo of'; alt on linked images describes destination
Comments: Alts are mostly dynamic product titles from APIs; no static checks prevent filenames, duplicate adjacent link text, or enforce 5–125 character bounds. product-compare uses empty alt for product images.

Checklist Item: Tables use thead and tbody
Comments: FPS comparison tables in product-card and product-matches include explicit <thead> and <tbody> sections.

Checklist Item: Table headers use th
Comments: Table header cells use <th scope="col"> / <th scope="row"> in FPS tables.

Checklist Item: Lists use ul or ol correctly
Comments: Many specs use native <ul>/<li>. Some UI uses div containers with role="list" (e.g. applied filters) instead of semantic <ul>, so list markup is not consistently native.

Checklist Item: Page <title> exists; is >10 characters; not a default value (Untitled/Document/Home/Page/Website/Welcome); follows consistent pattern across site (e.g. 'Page - Site' or 'Page | Site' separator detected)
Comments: head.html SEO script reads jcr:title for OpenGraph/Twitter/JSON-LD but never assigns document.title or injects a <title> element; repo head fragment lacks a static title tag, so HTML title consistency cannot be verified or enforced here.

Checklist Item: Meta viewport configured correctly
Comments: head.html declares <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" /> without user-scalable=no or maximum-scale restrictions.

Checklist Item: No duplicate IDs
Comments: No static analysis ensures globally unique ids; multiple blocks reuse generic ids such as budget-min-value/budget-max-value and compare-${sku} patterns that could collide if multiple instances mount.

Checklist Item: No invalid HTML nesting
Comments: No HTML validation step in npm scripts/CI; templates are string-built in JS without an automated nesting checker (e.g. vnu/html-validate).

Checklist Item: HTML validates without errors
Comments: No HTML validator integrated into the project scripts; no evidence of error-free W3C validation for generated markup.

Checklist Item: ARIA roles do not duplicate native semantics
Comments: At least one control uses <button> with role="link", mixing native button semantics with link role; this pattern is discouraged and can confuse assistive tech vs a real anchor.

Checklist Item: Avoid misuse of ARIA roles
Comments: Non-standard patterns found (e.g., <button role="link">, interactive divs with role button) without consistent keyboard semantics review; no lint rule prevents ARIA misuse.

Checklist Item: ARIA used only when necessary
Comments: Redundant ARIA appears on native list elements (e.g., <ul role="list"> in header cart) where the native semantics already convey role.

Checklist Item: Meta tags do not block zoom
Comments: Viewport meta uses width=device-width, initial-scale=1 without maximum-scale or user-scalable restrictions that would block zoom.

Checklist Item: No broken links (run broken-link-checker or linkinator CLI against local build or source HTML)
Comments: package.json has no broken-link-checker, linkinator, or similar link audit script; no CI step scans links in source.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for all colors
Comments: Global tokens are defined under :root in styles/styles.css (--color-* etc.), but multiple block stylesheets still use raw hex for colors (e.g. article-card, product-comparison, hero-banner, product-preview) instead of var() only.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for spacing scale
Comments: :root defines --spacing-0 through --spacing-64 in styles/styles.css and many rules use var(--spacing-*), but numerous block CSS files still use literal rem/px for padding, margin, and gap (e.g. 1.5rem, 44px, 2px) instead of the spacing token scale throughout.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for typography scale
Comments: styles/styles.css defines a typography token scale (--text-xxs through --text-5xl with matching line-height vars), but many block stylesheets set font-size with raw rem/px (e.g. 1.875rem, 13px, 12px) instead of var(--text-*) consistently; global heading rules in styles.css also use literal rem sizes.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded hex/rgb color values
Comments: Multiple CSS files contain hardcoded hex colors for properties (not only inside token definitions), e.g. article-card backgrounds, hero-banner #000, product-preview text colors.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded pixel spacing where tokens exist
Comments: Spacing tokens exist in styles/styles.css, but blocks still use arbitrary pixel and rem values (e.g. padding: 44px 0, gap: 2px, gap: 1.5rem) alongside some var(--spacing-*) usage, so hardcoded spacing remains common.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Block and global CSS largely follow consistent kebab-case class names and AEM-style cmp-* / BEM __ element naming (e.g. .cmp-header, .cmp-navigation__group). No camelCase class selectors found in sampled block CSS.

Checklist Item: Class names do not contain hex values or color names as identifiers; no purely positional names (left1/col2); BEM or project naming convention pattern enforced
Comments: Selectors reviewed use semantic/component names (cmp-*, columns, error-404) and BEM-style elements; no classes embedding hex literals or purely positional names like col2/left1 found in sampled CSS/HTML.

Checklist Item: CSS selectors for same component co-located in same file; no duplicate selectors scattered across files; related rules grouped by component
Comments: Header component selectors for .cmp-header and descendants appear in both blocks/header/header.css and a large duplicated section in styles/site.css (and aem-header.css), so the same component styling is not confined to a single block file.

Checklist Item: Layout containers use display:flex or display:grid; no float:left/right for multi-column layouts; no display:table for non-tabular content; absolute positioning in <5% of layout containers
Comments: Block CSS heavily uses flex/grid (many matches in blocks), but vendor styles/clientlib-base.css still contains numerous float:left rules and at least one display:table, so the repo does not fully avoid legacy float/table layout patterns.

Checklist Item: min-width media queries outnumber max-width queries; base styles (outside media queries) target mobile viewport dimensions
Comments: Repository-wide @media usage is dominated by max-width queries (229 occurrences) versus min-width/range min queries (35 + 12), indicating a max-width / desktop-down pattern rather than min-width-first mobile base styles.

Checklist Item: Standard breakpoints only
Comments: CSS uses many arbitrary pixel breakpoints (e.g. 680px, 688px, 730px, 820px, 925px, 1032px, 1117px) alongside common sizes like 768px and 1024px, so breakpoints are not limited to a small standard set.

Checklist Item: Responsive images by default
Comments: Global img rule sets max-width:100% and height:auto (styles/styles.css). Many blocks add loading="lazy", but dynamically rendered product/marketing images typically use a single src URL without srcset/sizes (e.g. product-card), so art-direction/DPR-responsive sources are not enforced by default in code.

Checklist Item: Avoid z-index escalation
Comments: Stylesheets include extremely large z-index values (e.g. 9999999) alongside many intermediate layers (99999, 10000, 1000), indicating z-index escalation rather than a small controlled stacking scale.

Checklist Item: Avoid expensive box-shadows
Comments: box-shadow usage is moderate in count and typically single-layer soft elevations (e.g. 4–24px blur). No evidence of widespread multi-layer or very large blur spreads that would be classically expensive.

Checklist Item: Avoid heavy CSS filters
Comments: Several components apply large blur filters (e.g. blur(32px) on cards/headers) and styles/styles.css includes blur(150px) filters, which are expensive for paint/compositing compared to light UI effects.

Checklist Item: No infinite CSS animations on non-loading elements; animation duration under 1s; total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: styles/styles.css defines non-loading infinite animations (e.g. rotateGradient 20s linear infinite) in addition to loader/spinner animations. Decorative infinite motion violates the checklist and some cycles exceed 1s.

Checklist Item: All CSS properties/values supported in target browsers (checked against caniuse compatibility data); no unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks
Comments: No repo artifacts show systematic cross-checking against caniuse data. CSS relies on relatively modern features such as :has() across many blocks without accompanying @supports fallbacks in the sampled files, so compatibility assurance is not demonstrated in code.

Checklist Item: Avoid vendor prefixes unless required
Comments: Several stylesheets still include legacy -webkit-box / -webkit-inline-box flexbox prefixes alongside standard display:flex, which is typically unnecessary for current evergreen targets and adds noise.

Checklist Item: No duplicated CSS rules
Comments: Large portions of component CSS are duplicated between block files and aggregated styles (e.g. .cmp-header rules appear in both blocks/header/header.css and styles/site.css with overlapping selectors), indicating duplicated rules rather than a single source of truth.

Checklist Item: No unused CSS selectors
Comments: No automated unused-CSS verification is present in-repo from this audit pass; large compiled bundles (styles/site.css) and a dated parallel stylesheet (styles-old__28-Nov.css) increase likelihood of unused selectors, but static usage was not exhaustively proven.

Checklist Item: Consistent property ordering
Comments: No stylelint plugin configuration for declaration ordering (e.g. stylelint-order) is present in the repo, and CSS blocks commonly interleave legacy prefixed properties with unprefixed ones in non-uniform ways.

Checklist Item: No blocking scripts in head
Comments: head.html loads several classic scripts without defer/async (e.g. /scripts/dompurify.min.js, /scripts/swiper-bundle.min.js) alongside module scripts; classic scripts in head parse/execute synchronously and block HTML parsing.

Checklist Item: Use async or defer appropriately
Comments: Same as blocking-scripts finding: non-module scripts in head.html (dompurify.min.js, swiper-bundle.min.js) are included without async/defer attributes, so they are not loaded with non-blocking hints.

Checklist Item: Third-party scripts lazy loaded
Comments: GTM is injected dynamically with async in scripts/google-data-layer.js, but other third-party bundles (e.g. dompurify.min.js, swiper-bundle.min.js) are loaded directly from head.html without lazy/on-demand loading strategies.

Checklist Item: No JS files loaded on pages where their exports are unused; code-split bundles match route usage
Comments: head.html wires multiple global scripts (aem.js, scripts.js, carousel stack, configs.js, google-data-layer.js, utils.js) for all pages without route-based splitting; there is no evidence each export is tree-shaken per route.

Checklist Item: No marketing tags in head
Comments: head.html does not embed marketing tag manager script URLs directly; analytics loading is handled via ES modules (e.g. google-data-layer.js) rather than inline third-party tag snippets in the head markup.

Checklist Item: User-facing strings in component render/template functions reference i18n keys or constants; hardcoded display strings >3 words flagged
Comments: Block JS contains many long hardcoded English UI strings and default copy in objects/templates (e.g. product-listing filter helper text) rather than indirection through i18n keys or shared string constants.

Checklist Item: No binaries stored in repository
Comments: Repository scan under the project tree (excluding node_modules) found no common compiled binary artifacts (.exe, .dll, .so, .dylib, .jar, .wasm); assets are web media formats.

Checklist Item: Error messages follow consistent pattern (checked via regex); no raw string throws; error constants or error classes used
Comments: Codebase uses ad-hoc throw new Error('...') string literals (e.g. scripts/api-service.js, aem-header/footer) rather than a centralized error factory/constants or typed error classes, so throws are not standardized beyond plain Error messages.

Checklist Item: Avoid tight DOM coupling
Comments: Block scripts frequently assume specific DOM shapes (e.g. header.js uses deep selectors like 'div > div > div' and fixed ids/classes), tightly coupling JS to authored markup rather than abstracting behind data attributes or stable component APIs.

Checklist Item: Avoid forced synchronous layouts
Comments: Layout metrics (offsetHeight, getBoundingClientRect) are read in multiple blocks during interactions without clear batching via requestAnimationFrame everywhere; limited use of ResizeObserver/IntersectionObserver, so forced synchronous layout risks are not systematically mitigated in code review.

Checklist Item: All setTimeout/setInterval calls have corresponding clear calls; timer count per file within threshold (max 3); no timers <100ms
Comments: Multiple project files use short-delay timers (e.g. 0ms/50ms setTimeout) and there are many timer calls across blocks without an audited guarantee each id is cleared; vendor swiper-bundle also embeds frequent timers.

Checklist Item: setInterval for DOM/state checks flagged (suggest MutationObserver/IntersectionObserver/EventListener); polling intervals <1s flagged
Comments: No setInterval usage was found in first-party blocks/ and core scripts/*.js sources scanned; timers appear primarily as setTimeout and observers elsewhere, so DOM polling via setInterval is not present in project-authored code.

Checklist Item: Prefer native browser APIs
Comments: First-party code uses modern ES modules, fetch/DOM APIs, and observers without jQuery/lodash-style wrappers; remaining third-party bundles (swiper, dompurify) are focused libraries rather than broad DOM polyfills.

Checklist Item: Modern ES6+ syntax used
Comments: First-party sources are authored as ES modules (import/export), async/await, const/let, and template literals; legacy var usage appears only inside vendored minified bundles, not block implementations.

Checklist Item: Avoid memory leaks
Comments: Blocks register many addEventListener calls while removeEventListener is rarely used in comparison, suggesting long-lived listeners without teardown patterns for dynamic blocks; leak risk cannot be ruled out.

Checklist Item: Event listeners cleaned up properly
Comments: Consistent cleanup is not evident: removeEventListener usage is minimal compared to widespread addEventListener registrations across blocks, and few components show symmetric unsubscribe logic.

Checklist Item: Async logic uses async/await
Comments: Async flows predominantly use async/await in blocks; promise-chain .then usage is rare (single match in scan), indicating modern async style for network/block initialization.

Checklist Item: Promises handled correctly
Comments: Network helpers like scripts/api-service.js wrap fetch in try/catch, validate response.ok/content-type, and rethrow after logging; async block code generally awaits promises rather than ignoring rejections in reviewed samples.

Checklist Item: State changes occur through defined setters/reducers/actions; no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer
Comments: UI state is managed imperatively in block modules (mutating objects/element properties directly) without a centralized reducer/store pattern; no evidence of a single state layer mediating all updates.

Checklist Item: No mutation of shared state
Comments: Blocks assign and read globals on window (e.g. window.__productPreviewData) and rely on shared window.* libraries, which constitutes cross-feature shared mutable state outside an isolated store.

Checklist Item: No side effects in pure functions
Comments: Block helpers commonly interleave computation with DOM mutations and module-level side effects; there is no project-wide convention marking pure functions or isolating side effects, so purity cannot be assured.

Checklist Item: Consistent return types
Comments: Public helpers mix return shapes (sometimes returning undefined early with bare return;, sometimes arrays/objects, sometimes JSON blobs) without uniform Result-style typing across modules.

Checklist Item: No global variables
Comments: App code attaches many symbols to window (e.g. AEMHeader/AEMFooter, cart/SSO handlers, __productPreviewData, allProducts, renderProductCard). That is intentional global namespace use for AEM blocks, not an absence of globals.

Checklist Item: JS scoped to component or block
Comments: Block JS follows EDS layout: each block under blocks/<name>/*.js uses ES module import/export and file-level scope (e.g. modal exports createModal; header/footer use imports). Cross-block APIs use explicit window hooks but core logic stays module-scoped.

Checklist Item: Consistent coding style
Comments: Tooling enforces style: package.json defines eslint (Airbnb base, security/import plugins) and stylelint for blocks/scripts/constants; husky + lint-staged auto-fix on commit. Artifact docs/eslint-report.json confirms CI-style lint runs.

Checklist Item: Avoid deeply nested callbacks
Comments: Async flow uses await heavily across blocks/scripts (many more await than .then); quick scan found no chained .then pyramids. Callback nesting limited to normal DOM iteration patterns.

Checklist Item: Call chain depth <=4 levels; wrapper functions that only delegate flagged; files with >80% re-exports flagged
Comments: No automated enforcement (no eslint/rule or script scanning call depth, thin wrappers, or re-export-heavy files). App source has few barrel-style re-export files; chains are typical ESM—not proven <=4 globally. Requirement is procedural, not evidenced in toolchain.

Checklist Item: All external inputs are validated, sanitized, and securely handled to prevent errors, misuse, and security vulnerabilities
Comments: Sanitization is partial: product-listing uses DOMPurify; other blocks use escapeHtml in places. Many innerHTML assignments still splice API/strings without encoding (e.g. product-preview template uses game.gameTitle/imageUrl straight into innerHTML), so not all external data is uniformly validated/sanitized.

Checklist Item: No unreachable code
Comments: eslint CI report (docs/eslint-report.json) shows zero no-unreachable findings across linted files; Airbnb config includes unreachable detection. No manual dead branches spotted in spot checks.

Checklist Item: No empty catch blocks
Comments: head.html contains a catch that swallows JSON parse failures with only a comment (no logging or rethrow), which is effectively an empty error-handling block for that path.

Checklist Item: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance met
Comments: No automated WCAG AA verification artifacts or test suite are present in-repo from this pass; prior findings (e.g. swallowing errors in head metadata script, complex custom components) mean full AA conformance cannot be confirmed from static review alone.

Checklist Item: Announce dynamic content changes
Comments: Several blocks implement aria-live regions for specific flows (product listing announcements, compare errors, newsletter responses), but dynamic updates are not consistently announced across all interactive components.

Checklist Item: Modal/dialog elements have role='dialog' or role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby/aria-label; keydown handler for Escape key present in source; focusable elements exist inside dialog container
Comments: blocks/modal/modal.js sets role=dialog, aria-modal=true, and closes on Escape, but the dialog container lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label naming the dialog itself (only the close button has an aria-label).

Checklist Item: <video> elements have <track kind='captions'> with valid non-empty src
Comments: No <track kind='captions'> markup was found in block JS templates; video elements are built without accompanying caption tracks in the searched sources.

Checklist Item: <audio> elements have adjacent transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content; transcript container visible (not display:none/visibility:hidden)
Comments: No <audio> elements are authored in block JavaScript sources, so transcript linkage requirements do not apply to current UI code paths.

Checklist Item: No focus-trap calls outside of modal/dialog components; no keydown handlers that programmatically restrict Tab key to a subset of elements on non-modal pages
Comments: product-comparison implements custom Tab/Shift+Tab handling on non-modal hover panel content, programmatically moving focus among a subset of focusables, which goes beyond typical modal focus trapping patterns.

Checklist Item: Visible focus styles present
Comments: Multiple stylesheets set outline: none on interactive-looking selectors; while some components add alternative focus visuals, widespread outline removal increases risk of missing visible focus compared to a consistent :focus-visible ring.

Checklist Item: All CSS :hover rules have corresponding :focus or :focus-visible rules on the same selectors
Comments: Sampled blocks/header/header.css contains multiple :hover rules but no :focus or :focus-visible counterparts in the same file, so hover-only styling is not paired with keyboard-focus equivalents.

Checklist Item: Visually-hidden patterns (.sr-only/.visually-hidden) use approved clip/position technique; no display:none or visibility:hidden on screen-reader-targeted text
Comments: .sr-only/.visually-hidden utilities in styles/styles.css use the standard off-screen clipping pattern (absolute positioning, 1px box, overflow hidden, clip) rather than display:none for visually hidden text.

Checklist Item: No eval() or Function(string) usage
Comments: No eval() or new Function(string) usage was found in first-party blocks/, scripts/, or constants/ sources when excluding vendored bundles.

Checklist Item: No document.write or document.writeln
Comments: No document.write/document.writeln calls were found in project JavaScript sources outside dependency artifacts.

Checklist Item: External scripts use Subresource Integrity (SRI) where applicable
Comments: Third-party GTM is injected dynamically without integrity/crossorigin SRI attributes (scripts/google-data-layer.js), and head.html references local scripts without SRI hashes (typical for same-origin assets but does not meet SRI for applicable external loads).

Checklist Item: No unsanitized user input in innerHTML or dangerous sinks
Comments: Multiple sinks assign authored/API strings via innerHTML without universal encoding/DOMPurify (e.g. product-preview interpolates gameTitle/imageUrl; broad innerHTML usage across blocks—see grep). Partial escapeHtml/DOMPurify exists but not enforced on all sinks.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source
Comments: Scanned JS/JSON/HTML for common secret patterns (sk-, long Bearer tokens, aws keys, BEGIN keys, client_secret); no hits. configs load from /configuration.json; constants/api-constants.js only exposes public path constants.

Checklist Item: No credentials or tokens in URLs or query parameters
Comments: Auth tokens (Magento) and cart payloads are sent via JSON POST bodies to /bin/asuscto/* endpoints—not embedded in URLs or URLSearchParams. Filter APIs only pass websiteCode/sorting/pagination in query.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data in client-side comments or console logs
Comments: console.log/error usage is sparse; grepped for token/password/secret—no dumps of credentials. Comments reference token flow generically without values. Remaining logs are UI/debug (menu click metadata, scores) not secrets.

Checklist Item: No insecure storage usage (localStorage/sessionStorage/Cookies or other browser storage for secrets)
Comments: sessionStorage persists Magento bearer token (`cto_token_us`), cart IDs, guest cart payloads, and login/username flags—recoverable via XSS/extension. Requirement disallows storing secrets in browser storage; implementation uses storage for ecommerce session continuity.

Checklist Item: No PII stored in cookies or browser storage without encryption or consent
Comments: Auth ticket (`aticket` cookie via getCookie/deleteCookie), guest/session IDs (`asus_mg_guest_id`), and `userName` in sessionStorage are stored in cleartext without client-side encryption; no consent/logging layer in code reviewed. Requirement not met.

Checklist Item: No known vulnerable dependencies (npm audit / Snyk)
Comments: Ran `npm audit` in workspace: reports 7 known issues ( lodash/picomatch/postcss/yaml/brace-expansion transitive — dev tooling paths) with highs/moderates; fixes suggested via npm audit fix. Per policy, unresolved advisories ⇒ No.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data exposed in client-visible IDs or data attributes
Comments: Searched DOM data-* usage: blocks store product/catalog JSON in dataset (e.g., data-product/data-config) but no evidence of auth cookies, tickets, tokens, or passwords pushed into attributes. Remaining attributes are UI metadata (counts, swiper indexes).

Checklist Item: Zero lint errors (ESLint/Stylelint)
Comments: Parsed docs/eslint-report.json: 57 files linted with aggregate 120 ESLint errors and 79 warnings (examples include no-console, import rules). Not zero-error state ⇒ fails checklist.

Checklist Item: No unused variables
Comments: Across docs/eslint-report.json ESLint snapshot, automated scan shows zero findings whose ruleIds mention unused vars (Airbnb disables/warn differs but none surfaced). Matches “no unused variables” for linted corpus.

Checklist Item: No unused functions
Comments: Standard ESLint setup (Airbnb) does not prove absence of dead functions—no ts-prune/unused-exports tooling in package.json, and eslint-report issues are dominated by other rules. Dead helper functions may exist without being flagged.

Checklist Item: No unused imports
Comments: docs/eslint-report.json shows zero `no-unused-vars` findings (imports count as bindings under Airbnb). Remaining ESLint violations are unrelated (console, formatting, imports order, etc.).

Checklist Item: No dead code paths
Comments: ESLint snapshot lacks no-unreachable/no-constant-condition/default-case findings, indicating major dead-path patterns are absent. Full branch coverage not proven, but automated static checks show none flagged.

Checklist Item: No dead code files (unreferenced modules)
Comments: Potentially unreferenced modules exist (e.g. blocks/footer/temp_footer.js has no imports from other sources in a quick repo grep), and there is no automated depcheck proving every file is reachable.

Checklist Item: No commented-out production code
Comments: Commented-out production template code remains in blocks (e.g. commented return strings for CTA markup in product-comparison), violating the no-commented-production-code hygiene expectation.

Checklist Item: No implicit globals
Comments: Sources are ES modules with import/export; no evidence of leaky assignments creating new globals without declaration in sampled blocks (window.* assignments are explicit).

Checklist Item: Consistent file structure
Comments: AEM Franklin-style structure is consistent: each block lives under blocks/<name>/ with paired JS/CSS and shared scripts/ + styles/ directories.

Checklist Item: No duplicated constants
Comments: Many `/bin/asuscto/*` paths are inlined in scripts/api-service.js (checkTicket/getCart/addToCart/...) instead of consolidating with constants/api-constants.js. Same literals appear scattered, failing DRY/no duplicated constants guideline.

Checklist Item: Variable names >2 characters (excluding loop iterators i/j/k/x/y); no single-letter variables outside loops; no anti-pattern names (temp/tmp/data/val/stuff/thing/foo/bar without qualifier); camelCase or snake_case consistent per file
Comments: Multiple block/util files declare single-letter locals outside tight loops (e.g. `const n` for parsed numbers, footer `const a` anchors, tooltip `t/l`, product-compare `let t`). Violates “>2 chars outside loop iterators” naming rule despite overall camelCase consistency.

Checklist Item: Function names start with verb (get/set/is/has/should/create/update/delete/handle/render/fetch/parse/validate/transform/format/check/find/load/save/remove/add/init/build/compute/on); names >4 characters; no generic names (doStuff/handleIt/process/run without descriptive suffix)
Comments: Sampled header/helpers: verbs like fetchCartProducts, mergeGuestCartToMember, renderMiniCartContent, handleAddToCart; no doStuff/handleIt patterns found. EDS default export `decorate` is framework convention and widely used—acceptable domain verb.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Predominantly camelCase ESM naming, but legacy/typo files remain (`blocks/footer/uifrontned_footer.js`, duplicated `temp_footer.js`, mixed uifrontend_* spelling) harming uniform conventions.

Checklist Item: No nested ternary expressions beyond 1 level; no if/else chains >4 branches (suggest switch/object-map); boolean expressions <=3 conditions per statement; complex boolean expressions without intermediate named variable flagged
Comments: Limited automated pattern checks: no obvious nested-ternary matches in large modules (e.g. product-listing); else-if counts in megabyte files stay modest in samples. Boolean complexity not exhaustively measured—no clear systematic violations found.

Checklist Item: Functions with if-block wrapping >80% of function body flagged for guard-clause inversion; nested if-else >3 levels deep where outermost condition is validation/type-check flagged for early return
Comments: No lint rule/script flags guard-clause inversion or overly nested validation ladders; codebase contains deep DOM logic but requirement calls for tooling-based flagging—not present.

Checklist Item: Average file size under 500 LOC; functions under 40 lines; module boundaries well-defined
Comments: Numerous block/service files exceed 500 LOC (product-matches ~1575, header ~1540, help-me-choose ~1224, footer ~1047, listing ~884, etc.), so average file size/function length targets are not met.

Checklist Item: Files have single responsibility and clear module boundaries
Comments: Largest modules (product-matches, header, help-me-choose, listing) intertwine fetching, templating, state, analytics, accessibility—beyond single-purpose boundaries despite one-block-per-file heuristic.

Checklist Item: README present
Comments: Repository root ships README.md (Adobe Helix boilerplate documentation) outlining setup and usage.

Checklist Item: Consistent error handling strategy
Comments: Error paths mix logging+empty fallbacks (`fetchFilters` returns []), thrown Errors for auth flows, silent DOM try/catches, and user-visible defaults per block—no single shared helper or taxonomy (typed errors/retry/UI messaging). Strategy is inconsistent repo-wide.

Checklist Item: Errors logged meaningfully
Comments: Network layer (scripts/api-service.js) uniformly logs failures via console.error with context snippets; decorator blocks similarly log parse failures (404 block, configs). Silent catches are uncommon in sampled paths—overall logging adequate though not centralized.

Checklist Item: Errors fail gracefully
Comments: Key blocks degrade to authored/default UI when upstream data missing (listing placeholder copy, preview shows “Product data not available”, 404 parses safely). Exceptions still bubble in some commerce calls but storefront surfaces generally avoid hard blank screens.

Checklist Item: Avoid magic numbers
Comments: Defaults embed unexplained literals (`pageSize = 6`, `minBudget/maxBudget 500/5000`, `timeoutMs = 5000`, hardcoded publish host fallback). No shared named constants file for these budgets/timeouts—classic magic numbers remain.

Checklist Item: Constants centralized
Comments: Some constants live in constants/api-constants.js, yet many literals (timeouts, budgets, assorted `/bin/asuscto/*` URLs) remain hardcoded throughout scripts/api-service.js and UI blocks.

Checklist Item: Config-driven behavior
Comments: Runtime loads `/configuration.json` via scripts/configs.js/getConfigValue for endpoints, AppID, locale, etc., and block JSON models drive content—commerce behavior is largely config-driven instead of hardcoded only.

Checklist Item: Shared utilities extracted
Comments: Common logic centralized under scripts (aem.js, scripts.js helpers, configs, api-service, cookie, google-data-layer) and reused from blocks via ES module imports despite some duplicated parsing helpers per block.

Checklist Item: Duplicate code percentage within standard threshold
Comments: No duplicate-code telemetry (no jscpd/Sonar report checked in docs); heuristic shows repeated numeric parsing helpers duplicated across listing/help-me-choose/product-sidebar snippets. Threshold not evidenced.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded environment values
Comments: Fallback `getConfigValue('api-endpoint') || 'https://publish-p165753-e1767020.adobeaemcloud.com'` embeds a concrete publish host directly in scripts/api-service.js; other blocks also reference fixed dam paths—environment not fully externalized.

Checklist Item: Avoid circular dependencies
Comments: Ran `npx madge --circular scripts blocks` locally; tool reports ✔ No circular dependency found covering 52 graph nodes.

Checklist Item: No stale TODO/FIXME comments; no comments referencing symbols that do not exist in scope; no commented-out code blocks
Comments: TODO comments remain in scripts/scripts.js without issue references, and commented-out production snippets exist elsewhere; stale TODO guidance not met.

Checklist Item: commented-out code blocks >3 lines flagged; TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker reference flagged;
Comments: Same findings as adjacent row: TODOs lack ticket references (scripts/scripts.js) and multi-line commented-out templates exist in blocks, so the stricter TODO/comment hygiene item is not satisfied.

Checklist Item: Charset meta tag (<meta charset="UTF-8">) declared in <head> before any content-producing elements; charset declaration appears within first 1024 bytes of document
Comments: head.html fragment does not include <meta charset="UTF-8"> (grep shows no charset declaration), so the HTML head template does not itself guarantee early charset placement.

Checklist Item: Language attribute (lang) on <html> element is a valid BCP 47 tag matching page content language; lang attribute not empty or set to 'undefined'
Comments: 404.html ships <html> without a lang attribute; other pages rely on head.html runtime setPageLanguage from metadata rather than a guaranteed static BCP-47 lang in the source fragment.

Checklist Item: Direction attribute (dir) specified on <html> or relevant container elements for RTL language pages; value is 'ltr' or 'rtl' matching content language direction
Comments: No explicit dir attributes are authored in sampled HTML fragments; for LTR-only content the HTML user-agent default direction applies, and no RTL-specific templates requiring explicit dir were found in-repo.

Checklist Item: If CSS print stylesheet (@media print) is defined, print styles hide non-essential UI elements (navigation/footer/sidebar/ads)
Comments: A @media print block exists in styles/styles-old__28-Nov.css but only forces monochrome/background removal and typography breaks; it does not hide navigation/footer/sidebar chrome for printing.

Checklist Item: Font-family declarations defined at :root or body level using CSS custom properties; component-level stylesheets reference font-family tokens instead of hardcoding font names
Comments: :root defines --font-* tokens in styles/styles.css and sampled block CSS uses var(--font-text)/var(--font-display) rather than hardcoded family stacks in component files.

Checklist Item: Content elements have explicit width and height HTML attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set to reserve layout space and prevent CLS
Comments: Many dynamically rendered tags omit width/height attributes and rely on CSS without guaranteed aspect-ratio on all product imagery (e.g. product-card templates), so CLS reservation is not consistently implemented.

Checklist Item: No unused npm/yarn packages in package.json; depcheck or npm-check analysis confirms all listed dependencies are imported in source code
Comments: package.json does not include depcheck/npm-check scripts or evidence that every dependency is referenced from source; manual verification was not performed for all packages.

Checklist Item: No setState/dispatch/state-mutation calls inside for/while/do-while/forEach/map/reduce loops; state updates batched or moved outside iteration blocks
Comments: Imperative code frequently mutates DOM/state inside forEach/map loops (e.g., building markup per item) rather than batching updates; there is no React-style setState, but the checklist’s batching intent is not consistently followed.

Checklist Item: No unused component props passed from parent to child; unused prop detection via static analysis or TypeScript strict mode
Comments: Project is AEM EDS decorate blocks manipulating DOM—no React parent/child prop drilling. Unused-prop linting / TS strict irrelevant; nothing to statically analyze.

Checklist Item: Git repository initialized with .git directory present; remote origin configured; repository is not in detached HEAD state
Comments: `git rev-parse --git-dir` fails in workspace (no .git metadata). Remote/origin and branch state therefore unverifiable here—treat as not meeting checked-in VCS requirement locally.

Checklist Item: .gitignore file present and includes standard exclusions (node_modules; dist/build output; .env files; OS files; IDE config); no build artifacts or dependency folders committed
Comments: No `.gitignore` file present under audit workspace (Glob/test -f confirm). Cannot assert standard exclusions (node_modules, dist, env) are enforced.

Checklist Item: Package lock file (package-lock.json or yarn.lock) committed to repository; lock file version matches package.json dependencies; no conflicting lock files present
Comments: `package-lock.json` present (214 KB), no conflicting `yarn.lock`/`pnpm-lock`. Lock tracks package.json deps; standard npm pinning satisfied for this audit snapshot.

Checklist Item: Pre-commit hooks configured (husky; lint-staged; or lefthook); hooks run linting and formatting before each commit; .husky directory or equivalent present
Comments: `package.json` lists `husky` + `lint-staged`, but workspace lacks `.husky/` hooks directory (glob). Without committed hook scripts, mandatory pre-commit automation not demonstrated.

Checklist Item: Node.js version specified via engines field in package.json; version is LTS or current stable
Comments: package.json lacks an `engines` constraint for Node/npm, so toolchain version/LTS pinning is undocumented despite devDependencies tooling.

Checklist Item: Hardcoded mock data or example values left in production code (e.g. 'John Doe'; 'test@example.com'; 'Lorem ipsum'; '123 Main St'; placeholder UUIDs); regex pattern scan across source files
Comments: Regex scan for common placeholders (Lorem, John Doe, test@example.com, fake UUIDs, 123 Main) returned no hits in source/HTML/JSON excluding lock/reports. Occurrences of “mock*” are DOM mockup fragments, not seeded personal data.
